# Intact Research
> [!tip] This is the last role being filled in the CDO leadership team. Greenfield mandate. People-first CDO. You've already passed screening. Next round is with Indhira Mani and panel.

---

## Who They Are

Intact Insurance UK is the rebranded RSA Insurance — officially rebranded October 2025 after Intact Financial Corporation (Canada's largest P&C insurer) acquired RSA in 2021 for £8 billion. The UK entity also absorbed NIG and FarmWeb from Direct Line Group in 2024, consolidating into a single commercial and specialty lines operation.

**Scale:**
- Parent: Intact Financial Corporation — CA$24 billion annual direct premiums, 31,000 employees globally
- UK ambition: grow from current base to £5 billion GWP by 2030 (Commercial and Specialty Lines)
- Over £1 billion already invested in UK&I since acquisition
- Underwriting profit doubled in 2024: £172M (up from £90M in 2023), combined ratio improved to 92.8%

**Strategic positioning:**
- Exited personal lines (sold to Admiral Group)
- Doubled down on commercial and specialty: property, construction, marine, agriculture, liability, professional & financial lines, casualty, rail, real estate, renewable energy
- Operates through regional and national brokers, wholesalers, and MGAs
- Global network: local expertise in 150+ countries

**Ambition in one line:** Become the UK's best commercial insurer by leveraging Intact's global data and AI capabilities.

---

## The Org Structure That Matters

```
CEO — Ken Norgrove (UK & Ireland)
  └── CIO — Oliver Holden
        └── CDO — Indhira Mani  ← Your boss
              ├── Data & Analytics
              ├── ML Platforms
              ├── Data Management & Foundation
              └── Director AI ← YOU (last hire to complete the team)
```

**Key: CDO reports into CIO, not risk/compliance.** This is a technology-focused data function, not a defensive compliance function. That framing matters — AI here is about growth and competitive advantage, not just risk mitigation.

---

## The CDO — Indhira Mani (Know Her Deeply)

Appointed late summer 2024. Joined from a background spanning data leadership in financial services. This is your direct hiring manager's boss and likely your panel interviewer.

**Her stated philosophy (from her own public interview, March 2026):**

- "True transformation begins not with technology but with people"
- Data was historically viewed as a back-office function — she's repositioning it as a strategic growth lever
- Built cross-functional squads embedding data translators into underwriting, pricing, and claims
- Her 12-month success metric: the business saying "Can you do more of that?" — not frameworks or dashboards
- "Perfection can paralyse progress. Usability, delivered within regulatory guardrails, drives momentum"
- On AI: augmentation not replacement — "humans working alongside AI to make better decisions without fatigue or bias"
- Contrarian on centralisation: "Not all data needs to be centralised. Adoption matters more than architecture purity"
- Believes data must be the "offensive strategy" — more submissions, more quotes, more policies, more premium, more profit

> [!tip] Match her language in interview. Lead with people, adoption, and tangible value — not platforms, architecture, or governance frameworks. Save the technical depth for when they probe.

---
